text.title: Solo Time Tracking Examples
text.summary: Worked examples — one clean Friday close, one antipattern the methodology catches.

example.title: Clean Friday close, 2 clients
example.note: Week 2026-W21. Toggl is the sole source. 37 raw hours captured at boundary. Gaps: 1 block of 45min on Wed labelled "client-a:review" by operator after gap-detector prompt. Write-offs: SALES 3h, ADMIN 2h, LEARNING 1.5h, BREAK 5h = 11.5h. Billable client-a 14h, client-b 6h = 20h. billable_pct_actual = 20/(20+11.5) = 0.635. Just under data_warning threshold. invoice_drafts: client-a $2030, client-b $870. validator PASS.

example.title: Multi-tool drift the methodology catches
example.note: Week 2026-W21. Operator uses Toggl on Mac (24h), Clockify on phone (18h), paper notebook for meetings (6h). Friday reconciliation: 2h spent diffing three sources. Aggregate says 48h billable on a 40h week — clearly double-counted; billable_pct_actual = 0.82. Validator BLOCKS: billable_pct_actual > 0.65 without data_warning AND multi-source detected → halt. Repair: designate Toggl as source; Clockify + paper become signal_only; reconciliation collapses to 15min next week.
